evaluation.manager_summary: Exemplary intake call where Sandra handled a spouse calling on behalf of the patient. She expertly navigated consent requirements, collected complete clinical and insurance information, correctly triaged high urgency (withdrawal seizure history), and set clear same-day follow-up. The agent demonstrated exceptional empathy by separately acknowledging the family member's own distress and maintaining warm, human connection throughout.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent immediately slowed down, acknowledged the caller's difficulty, validated his distress, and used his name. The tone was warm and human throughout, particularly in acknowledging the spouse's emotional burden separately.
Agent collected carrier name, member ID, and group number. Framed verification as next step toward help. Did not capture plan type explicitly, though this is a minor omission.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Consider adding plan type (HMO/PPO) to the insurance collection sequence for complete verification.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured substance type (alcohol), severity (fifth a day), current use status (drinking to manage withdrawal), withdrawal history (seizure), and prior treatment history (none). Stayed clearly in intake role without diagnostic language.

Agent correctly identified high urgency (seizure during withdrawal, ongoing drinking to prevent further withdrawal) and scheduled same-day callback within two hours. Appropriately recognized need for medical detox.

Agent handled consent and HIPAA framing expertly without being cold or legalistic, confirmed patient readiness, explained the need for direct consent, and separately acknowledged the family member's own distress and offered resources.
No objections were raised during this call. The caller was cooperative and motivated throughout.

Agent provided specific next step (callback from admissions team), clear timing (within two hours, by 2pm), clear ownership (admissions team), and confirmed both parties would be available for the callback.

Agent avoided all diagnostic language, made no outcome guarantees, and stayed strictly within intake scope. Used appropriate framing around medical necessity ('sign that he needs') without overstepping into clinical assessment.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Take a breath, Jacob. You're doing the right thing by calling. I can hear how hard this is.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Jacob, I want to acknowledge — this has clearly been frightening for you too. Spouses and families go through a lot when a loved one is struggling. How are you holding up?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And Jacob — what you're doing for your husband right now is an act of love. Please don't forget to take care of yourself too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: His own, through his employer. It's CoreFirst Insurance.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, I have his card. Member ID BP31407894, group GRP-575659.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Perfect. I have that noted. Here's what I'd like to do: I'll verify Jamie's benefits today and have someone from our admissions team call both of you

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: He was drinking a fifth a day at his worst. Since the ER he's cut back but he's still drinking to avoid withdrawal.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. So he's currently using to manage withdrawal symptoms — that's actually a sign that he needs medically supervised detox before any residential or outpatient program.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: No. This is the first time we're looking at this.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm so sorry you and Jamie have been through that. A seizure during withdrawal is serious and I want you to know you've called the right place. We have medically supervised detox.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: So he's currently using to manage withdrawal symptoms — that's actually a sign that he needs medically supervised detox

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'll verify Jamie's benefits today and have someone from our admissions team call both of you — ideally with him present — within two hours.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Before I go further — is Jamie aware that you're calling today?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Because Jamie is an adult, anything we do in terms of treatment will require his consent — and because he's already expressed that consent to you and wants this, that makes this call something we can
